name: Crossover Mutation (Gen 1)
description:
Semantic code search optimized for AI agents - BEST FOR: finding functions/classes by concept, understanding code relationships, exploring unfamiliar codebases. FASTER THAN: Grep for conceptual searches. USE WHEN: searching for functionality rather than exact text matches.

BEST FOR: Finding functions/classes, exploring codebases

Transform natural language questions into optimal search queries:

QUERY TIPS:
- Use 1-3 words: "error handling", "auth", "database"
- Concepts work best: "authentication" not file paths
- Natural language questions need transformation:
  "How does auth work?" → "authentication"
  "What handles errors?" → "error handler"

EXAMPLES:
  "How does authentication work?" → "authentication"
  "What handles errors?" → "error handler"
  "Find auth logic" → "authentication"
  "Where is WebSocket disconnect?" → "WebSocket disconnect"

AVOID:
- File paths: "src/auth.ts"
- Full sentences: "How do I handle errors?"
- Exact strings: "TODO", "FIXME"

❌ Avoid: Full sentences, questions, special chars
  - "How do I handle errors in the checkout?"
  - "function_that_validates_cart_items"
  - "src/cart/checkout.ts"

FILTERS: file_type, recency

SEARCH MODES:
- "fts" (full-text search): Best for exact keyword matches, identifiers
- "vector" (semantic search): Best for conceptual queries, similar code
- "hybrid" (default): Combines both for optimal results

FILTERS: Narrow by file_type, recency, repo_id, worktree_id
DEBUG: Set debug=true to see score breakdowns
notes: Generated via crossover mutation from 2 parent(s)
